 {"id":"bd-r79z","title":"GH#245: Windows MCP subprocess timeout for git rev-parse","description":"User reports git detection timing out on Windows in MCP server, but CLI works fine.

Path: C:\Users\chris\Documents\DEV_R\quarto-cli
Error: Git repository detection timed out after 5s
Works fine in CLI: `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` succeeds

Hypothesis: subprocess.run() with asyncio.to_thread() may have Windows-specific issues or the MCP runtime environment may not have proper PATH/git access.

Potential fixes:
1. Add subprocess shell=True on Windows
2. Increase timeout further for Windows
3. Add better error logging to capture subprocess stderr
4. Skip git resolution entirely on timeout and just use provided path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-07T16:31:37.531223-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T19:00:44.358543-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T19:00:44.358543-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-rb75","title":"Clean up merge conflict artifacts in .beads directory","description":"After resolving merge conflicts in .beads/beads.jsonl, leftover artifacts remain as untracked files:
- .beads/beads.base.jsonl
- .beads/beads.left.jsonl

These appear to be temporary files created during merge conflict resolution.

Options to fix:
1. Add these patterns to .beads/.gitignore automatically
2. Clean up these files after successful merge resolution
3. Document that users should delete them manually
4. Add a check in 'bd sync' or 'bd doctor' to detect and remove stale merge artifacts

Preferred solution: Add *.base.jsonl and *.left.jsonl patterns to .beads/.gitignore during 'bd init', and optionally clean them up automatically after successful import.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-06T19:09:16.114274-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-06T19:13:44.630402-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-06T19:13:44.630402-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-rbxi","title":"bd-hv01: Deletion tracking production readiness","description":"Epic to track all improvements and fixes needed to make the deletion tracking implementation ([deleted:bd-hv01]) production-ready.

The core 3-way merge algorithm is sound, but there are critical issues around atomicity, error handling, and edge cases that need to be addressed before this can be safely used in production.

Critical path (P1):
- Non-atomic snapshot operations
- Brittle JSON string comparison
- Silent partial deletion failures
- Race conditions in concurrent scenarios

Follow-up work (P2-P3):
- Test coverage for edge cases and multi-repo mode
- Performance optimizations
- Code refactoring and observability

Related commit: 708a81c","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-06T18:18:24.315646-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T03:12:04.15385-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T02:19:19.780741-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-rciw","title":"Support ephemeral protos: cook inline for pour/wisp/bond","description":"Gas Town moving to ephemeral protos (gt-4v1eo). Protos should be in-memory, not persisted beads. Changes: (1) bd cook outputs proto JSON to stdout by default, add --persist flag for old behavior. (2) bd pour/wisp cook inline from formula name. (3) bd mol bond accepts formula names, cooks inline. The low-level cookFormula() machinery stays for --persist use cases. See gt-4v1eo for full design. Related: bd-ia3g (rename ProtoID).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T14:30:40.782536-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T17:03:47.18261-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T17:03:47.18261-08:00"}
